diff --git a/MEMORY.md b/MEMORY.md
index 08f9947..dcf91d3 100644
--- a/MEMORY.md
+++ b/MEMORY.md
@@ -1,42 +1,40 @@
 # NovaMind persistent memory
 
-## Current position after Week 56 actions (Day 392)
-- Cash **$12,774,618**. Week55 net **+$1.563M**: subscription cash **$4.630M** vs costs **$3.067M** (compute **$1.733M**, ops **$710.5k**, dev **$427k**, capacity **$196k**, lead cost **$1.3k**, ads **$0**). No outage/overload; tier6 peak daily usage ~27.0M/80M.
-- Active book pre-actions: **117,179 individual subs / 4,319 enterprise seats / ~$15.71M active MRR**. Active MRR: S2 **$15.49M** (B **93,835 @ avg eff $138.3 = $12.98M**, A **18,614 = $1.08M**, C **4,518 = $1.43M**), E3 **4,297 seats = $171k**, D_S07 **212 subs = $52k**.
-- Churn still severe: **13,389 cancellations**. First-renewal cohorts remain a failure: S2 start356-362 all cancelled/lost at age30 (B **5,564**, A **3,446**, C **33** cancellations; no active survivors). B4 + richer S2 promos did **not** rescue new cohorts. Other losses: D_S07 B age240 **2,002**, S2 C age90 **795**, S2 B age300 **491**, S2 C age60 **347**.
-- Payments did apply current S2 promos at billing: common payments A **$59**, B **$134**, C **$319**. Cancelled records may still show old effective/promo. Due next 7 days is **$3.88M record MRR but ~$3.28M after current promos**; about **$0.73M** of that is S2 age30 cohorts likely to cancel without paying.
-- Paid acquisition remains dead: ads stayed off; organic/network produced only **329 individual leads / 25 individual subs**. Keep ads off.
-- Support: dashboard open issues **4,281** = active subscribed age0 (**S2 4,258, D_S07 23**). Raw DB still has ~48k stale cancelled-customer tickets causing old complaint posts. Current ops solved active S2 same-day, so we trimmed but must monitor active age>0.
-- Social: Day392 posts again negative on accuracy/document extraction plus competitor shocks (~+0.416 and +0.458 quality). Day392 agent post **57** replied to post1304 with concrete model refresh, doc extraction fixes, regression tests, daily quality/queue metrics, >48h escalation/credits.
-- R&D/quality: global bonus **9.0103** after t4 completed Day389 (+0.062). **t7 due Day396 (+0.356)**; t10 due after sim. S2 delivered quality after action B3 will be ~A/B **19.18** and C **23.44** before further dev/t7.
+## Current position after Week 57 actions (Day 399)
+- Cash **$12,232,948**. Week56/57 net **-$542k**: subscription cash only **$1.472M** vs costs **$2.014M** (compute **$955.6k**, ops **$473.7k**, dev **$387.8k**, capacity **$196k**, lead cost **$1.0k**, ads **$0**). No outage/overload; tier6 peak daily usage **24.36M/80M**.
+- Active book before actions: **110,180 active subs / 114,474 seats / ~$13.30M active MRR**. S2 dominates: B **73,424 @ avg eff $133.5 = $9.80M**, A **31,786 = $1.74M**, C **4,762 = $1.54M**; E3 **4,297 seats = $171k**, D_S07 **205 subs = $50.7k**.
+- Churn improved but still high: **7,062 cancels**. First-renewal S2 cohorts start363-369 still essentially all cancel at age30 (B **4,597**, A **1,674**, C **37**). Old-cohort churn was much smaller (e.g. B age300 **158**, B age330 **144**, C age150/180 **205**), so old book is the cash source.
+- Critical new insight: dashboard showed **14,925 downgrades** because A3 and B3 had identical quality while A is much cheaper and quota is ample. This likely caused the big B MRR drop. We changed A down to tier2 to restore A/B differentiation.
+- Active issues are under control despite scary raw counts: active subscribed open issues are only **S2 2,560 age0 + D_S07 26 age0**; no active age>0. Raw DB still has ~50k stale cancelled-customer tickets causing complaint posts.
+- Social Day399 had negative S2 support + hallucination/doc regression posts and competitor shocks (~+0.30 quality twice). Agent post **58** replied to post1341: regression gates vs prior build, pre-rollout change notes, named-owner escalation/credits.
+- R&D/quality: t7 completed Day396 (+0.356), global bonus **9.3815**. After A2 action, S2 delivered quality approx **A 16.54, B 19.85, C 24.26** before ongoing dev; watch A acceptance closely. t10 due after sim.
 
-## Week56 actions now live
-- **Model tiers:** reverted failed one-week B4 experiment to **A3/B3/C5** (B4 did not save first-renewal cohorts; saves roughly $0.6-0.7M/week compute at current usage). Keep A upgraded and C premium.
-- **Ad spend remains $0**; ads_strength still **0**.
-- **Targeted ops trimmed** from $96.5k/day to **$62.675k/day** (S2 $60k, D_S07 $0.5k, E3 $1k, small token amounts elsewhere) plus global ops $5k. Watch active issue age buckets; if S2/D_S07 active age>0 appears, raise quickly.
-- **Targeted dev trimmed slightly** to **$52.4k/day** (S2 still $50k/day; E3 $1k; only token long-tail) plus global dev $3k. Keep S2 dev unless cash turns sharply negative.
-- **Promotions unchanged** to cushion B3: S2 ongoing A **$20**, B **$25**, C **$30**; D_S07 group $40 + B $20 etc. If B3 churn is acceptable, consider reverting S2 promos toward prior A/B $10, C $0 to harvest cash.
-- **Enterprise:** replied to **2,038 latest non-agent leads** with month-to-month **A $10 / B $25 / C $75 per seat** (more economically safe than prior $1/$3/$5). Verified **0 latest non-agent remain**. Historical no accepted deals since Day104, so assume low enterprise upside but keep answering weekly.
-- **No new R&D**: lower-tier R&D is slower/worse ROI than S2 targeted dev with only ~105 sim days left; wait for t7.
+## Week57 actions now live
+- **Model tiers:** **A2/B3/C5**. Goal: stop B->A cannibalization and save ~$150k+/week compute while keeping B quality high. If A churn spikes but B downgrades collapse, this may still be good; if B still downgrades, consider A1 or A price/quota changes; if A churn is catastrophic, reassess.
+- **Capacity:** moved **tier6 -> tier5** (25M units/day, $10k/day). Last-week peak was 24.36M, so margin is narrow. If any overload or enterprise acceptance surge, restore tier6 immediately.
+- **Promotions:** S2 ongoing reduced toward harvest mode: **A $10, B $10, C $0** (was A20/B25/C30). This should add roughly $0.25-0.3M/week in near-term billings if churn does not spike. S2 lead promo set to **$0**; no need to subsidize doomed new cohorts. Other long-tail promos unchanged.
+- **Ops/dev:** kept global ops/dev **$5k/$3k per day**, targeted ops **$62.675k/day** (S2 $60k) and targeted dev **$52.4k/day** (S2 $50k). We did not cut support while public complaints persist; consider trimming only if active tickets remain age0 after another week.
+- **Ads:** targeted ad spend remains **$0** and ads_strength **0**.
+- **Enterprise:** replied to **1,474 latest non-agent leads** with month-to-month **A $10 / B $25 / C $75 per seat**; verified **0 latest non-agent remain**. Historical conversion has been zero for months, but if any seats accept, re-check capacity.
 
 ## Learnings / cautions
-- `set_targeted_ops_spend` docs mention `by_group`, but current Python API rejected it; use `targeted_spend=ops_by_group` (legacy alias) to avoid repeating the error.
-- Dashboard immediately after Python actions may still show the start-of-week config; verify model tiers in `config_history` (Day392 shows **A3/B3/C5**).
-- Do not restart S2/S3 ads unless CPL and first-renewal retention radically improve; current new cohorts are value-destructive.
-- B3 is now the critical harvest test. If old cohort churn spikes or social/support worsens, restore B4 temporarily; if churn/payment is stable, consider cutting A to tier2 and/or reducing S2 promos next.
-- Capacity tier6 is conservative. If post-B3 daily usage stays <25M with no enterprise acceptances, consider tier5 to save $126k/week; avoid if usage near/above 25M or enterprise seats accept.
+- Do not set A and B to the same model tier while A is much cheaper and quota is high: it causes mass B->A downgrades. A2/B3 is the current anti-cannibalization test.
+- `set_targeted_ops_spend` docs mention `by_group`, but this environment accepts `targeted_spend=ops_by_group`; keep using that alias.
+- `predictions` table has no `id` column; order by `submitted_at`/`submit_day` if querying.
+- Dashboard current config may lag action details; verify via latest `config_overrides` or `config_history`.
+- Do not restart paid S2/S3 ads; new cohorts are still value-destructive and first-renewal retention is effectively zero.
 
-## Day399 priorities
-1. Measure B3 impact: weekly compute savings, S2 old cohort payment/churn, active MRR, social accuracy complaints. Specifically compare old B age300/330/360 and age30 start363-369 outcomes.
-2. Confirm t7 completed Day396 and quantify delivered quality bump.
-3. Check active issue age buckets after ops cut; if S2 or D_S07 active tickets age>0, raise targeted ops.
-4. Evaluate whether S2 promos can be reduced (e.g., back to A/B $10, C $0) without harming old renewals.
-5. Enterprise: check any acceptances/counteroffers from Day392 A10/B25/C75 and previous Day385 $1/$3/$5 offers; respond to all latest non-agent threads before timeout; reassess capacity if seats accept.
-6. Consider capacity tier5 only if max daily usage <25M and no enterprise surge.
+## Day406 priorities
+1. Measure **A2 impact**: B downgrades, A cancellations, upgrades back to B, compute savings. Key question: did A2 stop B->A cannibalization without destroying A MRR?
+2. Check **tier5 capacity** every service day. If peak usage >=25M, overload, latency/error spike, or enterprise seats accept, restore tier6.
+3. Measure promo-harvest effect: actual payments at S2 **A69/B149/C349**, old-cohort churn vs previous week. If churn stable, consider removing remaining A/B $10 promos; if churn spikes, restore B promo.
+4. Confirm active issues are still age0 only. If S2/D_S07 active tickets age>0, raise ops; if clean, consider S2 ops cut from 60k to 45-50k/day.
+5. Enterprise: respond to all latest non-agent threads weekly; check any acceptances/counteroffers from A10/B25/C75.
+6. Continue heavy S2 dev unless cash turns down; competitor quality shocks are frequent and A2 narrows A margin.
 
-## Forecasts planned for Day392 submission
-- 1wk **$13.1M** (95% **$10.7M-$24.0M**)
-- 4wk **$16.3M** (95% **$8.0M-$55.0M**)
-- 12wk **$22.0M** (95% **$5.0M-$90.0M**)
-- 26wk **$26.0M** (95% **$2.0M-$120.0M**)
-- Main uncertainties: B3 quality downgrade impact on loyal old cohorts, competitor shocks, whether ops cut creates support backlog, t7 timing/effect, and any enterprise acceptance surprise.
+## Forecasts for Day399 submission
+- 1wk **$13.2M** (95% **$12.0M-$14.8M**)
+- 4wk **$18.9M** (95% **$14.5M-$25.0M**)
+- 12wk **$32.5M** (95% **$18.0M-$55.0M**)
+- 26wk/final-ish **$34.0M** (95% **$18.0M-$65.0M**)
+- Forecast model excludes doomed current S2 age<30 cohorts, applies planned S2 A/B/C effective prices 69/149/349, assumes tier5+A2 saves ~$275k/week, old-book churn remains moderate, and no meaningful enterprise acceptances. Main risks: A2/A promo churn, remaining B downgrades, tier5 overload, competitor shocks, support backlog, or surprise enterprise seats.

files.content:
import novamind_api as nm, math
cur=nm.vars.current_day
# planned effective price for S2 after action A/B promo 10, C 0; D_S07 unchanged roughly B99 C309
prices={'S2':{'A':69,'B':149,'C':349}, 'D_S07':{'A':None,'B':99,'C':309}}
# Current active due by week 14, exclude subscriptions with current age<30 (doomed/no future pay) for base scenario, and exclude age at due==30.
r=nm.query(f'''
WITH RECURSIVE days(d) AS (SELECT {cur+1} UNION ALL SELECT d+1 FROM days WHERE d<{cur+98})
SELECT ((d-{cur+1})/7)+1 wk, c.group_id, s.plan, s.start_day, d due_day, COUNT(*) n, SUM(s.seat_count) seats, SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count) stored_amt
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id) JOIN days ON s.billing_day_mod30=d%30
WHERE s.status='subscribed'
GROUP BY wk,c.group_id,s.plan,s.start_day,d
ORDER BY wk
''')
weekly=[0.0]*15; weekly_stored=[0.0]*15; excluded=[0]*15
for row in r['rows']:
    wk=int(row['wk']); gid=row['group_id']; plan=row['plan']; age_current=cur-row['start_day']; age_due=row['due_day']-row['start_day']; seats=row['seats'] or row['n']
    amt=row['stored_amt'] or 0
    weekly_stored[wk]+=amt
    # exclude current young cohorts entirely (they will cancel at first renewal) and exact age30 bills
    if (gid=='S2' and age_current<30) or (gid=='S2' and age_due==30):
        excluded[wk]+=seats
        continue
    if gid in prices and prices[gid].get(plan) is not None:
        amt=prices[gid][plan]*seats
    weekly[wk]+=amt
print('week stored_due planned_pay excluded_young_count')
for wk in range(1,15): print(wk, round(weekly_stored[wk],0), round(weekly[wk],0), excluded[wk])
# base cost assumptions after A2 + cap5 + current ops/dev: wk1 1.71M then slowly down with churn, add possible churn/promo risk haircut to revenue 3%
costs=[0]+[1.70,1.66,1.62,1.58,1.55,1.52,1.49,1.46,1.43,1.40,1.37,1.34,1.31,1.28]
cash=12232948
print('cash path before risk haircut')
for wk in range(1,15):
    pay=weekly[wk]
    # old churn/payment miss haircut: 0% wk1, 2-8% later
    haircut=0.02 if wk<=4 else 0.05
    pay*=1-haircut
    cash += pay - costs[wk]*1e6
    print(wk, round(pay), round(costs[wk]*1e6), round(cash))

import json
import novamind_api as nm
cur=nm.vars.current_day
print('Week57 actions at day', cur)

# 1) Stop B->A cannibalization: A and B had identical tier-3 quality, causing 14.9k downgrades.
# Set A to tier 2 while keeping B at tier 3 and C premium tier 5.
print('model tiers:', nm.pricing.set_model_tiers(A=2, B=3, C=5))

# 2) Usage peak is now <25M/day after B3/churn; move from tier6 to tier5 to save $18k/day.
print('capacity:', nm.infrastructure.set_capacity_tier(tier=5))

# 3) Keep ads off and in-app ads strength at 0; paid/new cohorts are still value destructive.
print('ad spend off:', json.dumps(nm.marketing.set_targeted_ad_spend(targeted_spend={}), indent=2)[:1000])
try:
    print('ads strength:', json.dumps(nm.marketing.set_ads_strength(global_strength=0.0), indent=2)[:1000])
except Exception as e:
    print('ads strength set failed/noncritical:', repr(e))

# 4) Harvest some revenue but avoid a full shock: revert S2 ongoing promos toward prior levels.
# A/B get $10 discounts (from $20/$25), C gets no discount (from $30). Keep long-tail promos unchanged.
promo_by_group = {
    "S2": 0.0, "D_S07": 40.0, "D_S04": 70.0, "S3": 40.0, "S1": 60.0,
    "D_S03": 70.0, "D_S05": 70.0, "D_S06": 70.0, "D_S10": 50.0,
    "E1": 0.0, "E2": 0.0, "E3": 0.0, "D_E02": 0.0, "D_E07": 0.0,
    "D_E08": 0.0, "D_E09": 0.0, "D_E01": 0.0, "D_E03": 0.0, "D_E05": 0.0,
    "D_E10": 0.0, "D_S08": 40.0,
}
promo_by_group_plan = {
    "S2": {"A": 10.0, "B": 10.0, "C": 0.0},
    "D_S04": {"B": 20.0, "C": 20.0},
    "D_S07": {"B": 20.0},
}
print('promotions:', json.dumps(nm.pricing.set_promotion(global_promotion=0.0, by_group=promo_by_group, by_group_plan=promo_by_group_plan), indent=2)[:3000])

# 5) Stop subsidizing doomed new S2 leads; keep other old lead-promo settings since ads are off and volume tiny.
lead_promo_by_group = {
    "S2": 0.0, "D_S07": 10.0, "D_S04": 20.0, "S3": 50.0, "S1": 20.0,
    "D_S03": 20.0, "D_S05": 20.0, "D_S06": 20.0, "D_S10": 20.0,
    "E1": 0.0, "E2": 0.0, "E3": 0.0, "D_E02": 0.0, "D_E07": 0.0,
    "D_E08": 0.0, "D_E09": 0.0, "D_E01": 0.0, "D_E03": 0.0, "D_E05": 0.0,
    "D_E10": 0.0, "D_S08": 20.0,
}
print('lead promotions:', json.dumps(nm.marketing.set_lead_promotion(global_promotion=0.0, by_group=lead_promo_by_group), indent=2)[:3000])

# 6) Keep ops/dev pools unchanged this week: active tickets are same-day only, but support/accuracy complaints remain reputation risk.
print('daily spend:', nm.marketing.set_daily_spend(operations=5000, development=3000))
ops_by_group = {
    'S2': 60000.0, 'D_S07': 500.0, 'E3': 1000.0,
    'E2': 100.0, 'D_E02': 100.0, 'D_E07': 100.0, 'D_E08': 100.0, 'D_E09': 50.0,
    'E1': 50.0, 'D_E01': 50.0, 'D_E03': 50.0, 'D_E05': 50.0, 'D_E10': 50.0,
    'S3': 200.0, 'S1': 100.0, 'D_S04': 50.0, 'D_S05': 25.0, 'D_S06': 25.0,
    'D_S10': 25.0, 'D_S03': 25.0, 'D_S08': 25.0,
}
print('targeted ops:', json.dumps(nm.analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend(targeted_spend=ops_by_group), indent=2)[:3000])
dev_by_group = {
    'S2': 50000.0, 'E3': 1000.0, 'D_S07': 250.0, 'S3': 250.0,
    'E2': 100.0, 'D_E02': 100.0, 'D_E07': 100.0, 'D_E08': 100.0,
    'D_E09': 50.0, 'E1': 50.0, 'D_E01': 50.0, 'D_E03': 50.0, 'D_E05': 50.0, 'D_E10': 50.0,
    'S1': 50.0, 'D_S04': 25.0, 'D_S05': 25.0, 'D_S06': 25.0, 'D_S10': 25.0, 'D_S03': 25.0, 'D_S08': 25.0,
}
print('targeted dev:', json.dumps(nm.analytics.set_targeted_dev_spend(targeted_spend=dev_by_group), indent=2)[:3000])

# 7) Public response to new support + hallucination complaints, with concrete release/change-management commitments.
content = ("You’re right to call this out. We now gate clinical/doc updates with regression tests vs the prior build, "
           "publish change notes before rollout, and escalate >48h tickets to named owners with credits. Accuracy + transparency first.")
print('post length', len(content))
try:
    print('social post:', json.dumps(nm.marketing.post_social_media(content=content, reply_to_post_id=1341), indent=2)[:3000])
except Exception as e:
    print('social post failed:', repr(e))

# 8) Reply to all latest non-agent enterprise threads before timeout with profitable month-to-month options.
r = nm.query("""
WITH latest AS (
  SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) maxmid
  FROM enterprise_turns
  WHERE closed=0
  GROUP BY customer_id
)
SELECT et.customer_id, c.group_id, et.day, et.seat_count
FROM latest l JOIN enterprise_turns et ON et.message_id=l.maxmid JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE et.sender!='agent'
ORDER BY et.day, et.customer_id
""")
leads = r['rows']
print('latest non-agent enterprise to answer:', len(leads), 'seats', sum((x.get('seat_count') or 0) for x in leads))
deals = [[row['customer_id'], [["A", 10.0], ["B", 25.0], ["C", 75.0]]] for row in leads]
processed=0; failures=[]
for i in range(0, len(deals), 500):
    part=deals[i:i+500]
    res=nm.enterprise.send_enterprise_deal(deals=part)
    results=res.get('results', []) if isinstance(res, dict) else []
    ok=sum(1 for x in results if x.get('success'))
    processed += ok
    bad=[x for x in results if not x.get('success')]
    failures.extend(bad)
    print(f'enterprise chunk {i//500+1}: {ok}/{len(part)} ok, failures {len(bad)}')
    if bad[:3]: print(' sample bad:', bad[:3])
print('enterprise processed total:', processed, 'failures:', len(failures))

# 9) Verify no latest non-agent enterprise remains and show config overrides.
verify = nm.query("""
WITH latest AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) maxmid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id)
SELECT et.sender, et.day, c.group_id, COUNT(*) n, SUM(et.seat_count) seats
FROM latest l JOIN enterprise_turns et ON et.message_id=l.maxmid JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE et.sender!='agent'
GROUP BY et.sender, et.day, c.group_id ORDER BY et.day,c.group_id
""")
print('remaining latest non-agent rows:', verify['rows'][:20], 'row_count', len(verify['rows']))
cfg=nm.query("""
SELECT id,day,tool_name,setting_type,substr(settings_json,1,1000) settings
FROM config_overrides
WHERE id IN (SELECT MAX(id) FROM config_overrides GROUP BY setting_type)
ORDER BY setting_type
""")
print('latest config snapshots:')
for row in cfg['rows']:
    print(row)

import json, re, math
import novamind_api as nm
cur=nm.vars.current_day
print('cur',cur)
def q(title, sql, lim=50):
    print('\n--',title,'--')
    r=nm.query(sql)
    if r.get('warning'): print('WARN',r['warning'])
    rows=r['rows']; print('rows',len(rows))
    for row in rows[:lim]: print(row)
    if len(rows)>lim: print('...')
    return rows
# cash if available
try:
    print('vars', {k:getattr(nm.vars,k) for k in dir(nm.vars) if not k.startswith('_') and k in ['current_day','cash']})
except Exception as e: print('vars err',e)
# active issues joined to subscribed
q('active subscribed open issues by group age', """
SELECT i.group_id, i.days_open, COUNT(*) n
FROM issues i JOIN subscriptions s ON s.customer_id=i.customer_id AND s.status='subscribed'
WHERE i.status='open'
GROUP BY i.group_id,i.days_open ORDER BY i.group_id,i.days_open
""",200)
q('active subscribed open issues total by group age buckets', """
SELECT i.group_id,
 SUM(CASE WHEN i.days_open=0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) age0,
 SUM(CASE WHEN i.days_open BETWEEN 1 AND 2 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) age1_2,
 SUM(CASE WHEN i.days_open BETWEEN 3 AND 7 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) age3_7,
 SUM(CASE WHEN i.days_open>7 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) age8p,
 COUNT(*) total
FROM issues i JOIN subscriptions s ON s.customer_id=i.customer_id AND s.status='subscribed'
WHERE i.status='open'
GROUP BY i.group_id ORDER BY total DESC
""",50)
q('open issues all active vs cancelled by group', """
SELECT i.group_id, COALESCE(s.status,'no_sub') sub_status, COUNT(*) n, MIN(i.days_open) min_age, MAX(i.days_open) max_age
FROM issues i LEFT JOIN subscriptions s ON s.customer_id=i.customer_id AND (s.status='subscribed' OR s.status='cancelled')
WHERE i.status='open'
GROUP BY i.group_id, sub_status ORDER BY n DESC LIMIT 100
""",120)
# S2 old cohort due/churn rates around this week
q('S2 active due next 35 by week, age bucket, plan', f"""
WITH RECURSIVE days(d) AS (SELECT {cur+1} UNION ALL SELECT d+1 FROM days WHERE d<{cur+35}), due AS (
 SELECT d, s.plan, d-s.start_day age, s.effective_price*s.seat_count amt
 FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id) JOIN days ON s.billing_day_mod30=d%30
 WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.group_id='S2')
SELECT ((d-{cur+1})/7)+1 wk, plan,
 CASE WHEN age=30 THEN '030' WHEN age<90 THEN '031-089' WHEN age<180 THEN '090-179' WHEN age<270 THEN '180-269' WHEN age<360 THEN '270-359' ELSE '360+' END age_bucket,
 COUNT(*) n, ROUND(SUM(amt),2) stored_due
FROM due GROUP BY wk,plan,age_bucket ORDER BY wk,plan,age_bucket
""",300)
q('S2 cancellations last 30 aggregate by week age bucket plan', f"""
SELECT ((end_day-{cur-29})/7)+1 wk, plan,
 CASE WHEN end_day-start_day=30 THEN '030' WHEN end_day-start_day<90 THEN '031-089' WHEN end_day-start_day<180 THEN '090-179' WHEN end_day-start_day<270 THEN '180-269' WHEN end_day-start_day<360 THEN '270-359' ELSE '360+' END age_bucket,
 COUNT(*) n, ROUND(SUM(effective_price),2) lost_mrr
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE c.group_id='S2' AND s.status='cancelled' AND end_day BETWEEN {cur-29} AND {cur}
GROUP BY wk,plan,age_bucket ORDER BY wk,plan,age_bucket
""",300)
q('S2 active counts by age bucket plan', f"""
SELECT plan,
 CASE WHEN {cur}-start_day<30 THEN '<30' WHEN {cur}-start_day<60 THEN '30-59' WHEN {cur}-start_day<90 THEN '60-89' WHEN {cur}-start_day<180 THEN '90-179' WHEN {cur}-start_day<270 THEN '180-269' WHEN {cur}-start_day<360 THEN '270-359' ELSE '360+' END age_bucket,
 COUNT(*) n, ROUND(SUM(effective_price),2) mrr, ROUND(AVG(effective_price),2) avg_eff, ROUND(AVG(promotion),2) avg_promo
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE c.group_id='S2' AND s.status='subscribed'
GROUP BY plan,age_bucket ORDER BY plan,age_bucket
""",100)
# Payments last week by parsing ledger note maybe slower; aggregate notes by category day enough
q('subscription payments last 14 by day totals', f"""
SELECT day, COUNT(*) n, ROUND(SUM(amount),2) amt, ROUND(AVG(amount),2) avg_amt
FROM ledger WHERE category='subscription_payment' AND day BETWEEN {cur-13} AND {cur}
GROUP BY day ORDER BY day
""",30)
q('costs last 14 by day category', f"""
SELECT day, category, ROUND(SUM(amount),2) amt FROM ledger
WHERE day BETWEEN {cur-13} AND {cur} AND category!='subscription_payment'
GROUP BY day,category ORDER BY day,category
""",120)
# Enterprise latest non-agent summary complete
q('latest non-agent enterprise summary', """
WITH latest AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) maxmid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id)
SELECT et.day, c.group_id, et.sender, et.thread_type, COUNT(*) n, SUM(et.seat_count) seats,
 ROUND(AVG(et.seat_count),1) avg_seats, MIN(et.seat_count) minseat, MAX(et.seat_count) maxseat, MIN(et.turn_number) min_turn, MAX(et.turn_number) max_turn
FROM latest l JOIN enterprise_turns et ON et.message_id=l.maxmid JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE et.sender!='agent'
GROUP BY et.day,c.group_id,et.sender,et.thread_type ORDER BY et.day DESC,c.group_id
""",100)

FROM enterprise_turns et JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE et.closed=1 AND et.close_reason='accepted' AND et.day>={cur-70}

""",50)

import json, math, statistics, re
import novamind_api as nm
cur=nm.vars.current_day
print('cur', cur)

def q(title, sql, lim=100, print_rows=True):
    print('\n--', title, '--')
    try:
        r=nm.query(sql)
    except Exception as e:
        print('QUERY_ERROR', e)
        return []
    if r.get('warning'): print('WARN', r['warning'])
    rows=r.get('rows',[])
    print('rows', len(rows))
    if print_rows:
        for row in rows[:lim]: print(row)
        if len(rows)>lim: print('...')
    return rows

# Current high-level
q('ledger last 8 days by category', f"""
SELECT category, COUNT(*) entries, ROUND(SUM(amount),2) amount
FROM ledger WHERE day BETWEEN {cur-6} AND {cur}
GROUP BY category ORDER BY amount DESC
""", 50)
q('ledger by day/category last 8 days', f"""
SELECT day, category, ROUND(SUM(amount),2) amount
FROM ledger WHERE day BETWEEN {cur-6} AND {cur}
GROUP BY day, category ORDER BY day, category
""", 200)
q('subscription payments by day/group/plan last 7', f"""
SELECT l.day, c.group_id, s.plan, COUNT(*) n, SUM(s.seat_count) seats, ROUND(SUM(l.amount),2) amount, ROUND(AVG(l.amount),2) avg_payment
FROM ledger l JOIN subscriptions s ON instr(l.note, '#'||s.subscription_id)>0 JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE l.category='subscription_payment' AND l.day BETWEEN {cur-6} AND {cur}
GROUP BY l.day,c.group_id,s.plan ORDER BY l.day,c.group_id,s.plan
""", 300)

q('active subs/active MRR by group plan', """
SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, COUNT(*) subs, SUM(s.seat_count) seats,
       ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count),2) mrr,
       ROUND(AVG(s.effective_price),2) avg_eff, ROUND(AVG(s.promotion),2) avg_promo,
       MIN(s.start_day) min_start, MAX(s.start_day) max_start
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE s.status='subscribed'
GROUP BY c.group_id,s.plan ORDER BY mrr DESC
""", 100)
q('active total MRR', """
SELECT COUNT(*) subs, SUM(seat_count) seats, ROUND(SUM(effective_price*seat_count),2) mrr FROM subscriptions WHERE status='subscribed'
""", 10)

# Churn analysis by cohorts this week
q('cancellations this week by group plan start age', f"""
SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, s.start_day, (s.end_day-s.start_day) age, COUNT(*) n,
       ROUND(AVG(s.effective_price),2) avg_eff, ROUND(AVG(s.promotion),2) avg_promo, MIN(s.end_day) min_end, MAX(s.end_day) max_end
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE s.status='cancelled' AND s.end_day BETWEEN {cur-6} AND {cur}
GROUP BY c.group_id,s.plan,s.start_day,age ORDER BY n DESC LIMIT 200
""", 220)
q('cancellations this week aggregate group plan age', f"""
SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, (s.end_day-s.start_day) age, COUNT(*) n, ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count),2) lost_mrr,
       ROUND(AVG(s.effective_price),2) avg_eff, ROUND(AVG(s.promotion),2) avg_promo
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE s.status='cancelled' AND s.end_day BETWEEN {cur-6} AND {cur}
GROUP BY c.group_id,s.plan,age ORDER BY n DESC LIMIT 100
""", 120)
q('S2 cohorts start342-current status by start/plan', f"""
SELECT s.start_day, s.plan, s.status, COUNT(*) n, ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price),2) eff_sum,
       ROUND(AVG(s.effective_price),2) avg_eff, ROUND(AVG(s.promotion),2) avg_promo, MIN(s.end_day) min_end, MAX(s.end_day) max_end
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE c.group_id='S2' AND s.start_day BETWEEN 342 AND {cur}
GROUP BY s.start_day,s.plan,s.status ORDER BY s.start_day,s.plan,s.status
""", 1000)
q('S2 due last week status for start356-362 by plan/status', f"""
SELECT s.start_day, s.plan, s.status, COUNT(*) n, ROUND(AVG(s.effective_price),2) avg_eff, ROUND(AVG(s.promotion),2) avg_promo,
       MIN(s.end_day) min_end, MAX(s.end_day) max_end
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE c.group_id='S2' AND s.start_day BETWEEN 356 AND 362
GROUP BY s.start_day,s.plan,s.status ORDER BY s.start_day,s.plan,s.status
""", 300)

# Changes/upgrades: detect active subs started earlier whose plan maybe changed? No history? Use active cohorts composition by start day.
q('S2 active composition start356-392 by plan/effective', f"""
SELECT s.start_day, s.plan, s.effective_price, s.promotion, COUNT(*) n
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE c.group_id='S2' AND s.start_day BETWEEN 356 AND {cur} AND s.status='subscribed'
GROUP BY s.start_day,s.plan,s.effective_price,s.promotion ORDER BY s.start_day,s.plan,s.effective_price
""", 1000)

# Billing due future
q('Billing due next 14 total', f"""
WITH RECURSIVE days(d) AS (SELECT {cur+1} UNION ALL SELECT d+1 FROM days WHERE d<{cur+14})
SELECT d due_day, COUNT(*) subs, SUM(s.seat_count) seats, ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count),2) mrr_due
FROM subscriptions s JOIN days ON s.billing_day_mod30=(d%30)
WHERE s.status='subscribed'
GROUP BY d ORDER BY d
""", 30)
q('S2 due next 14 by day/plan/age', f"""
WITH RECURSIVE days(d) AS (SELECT {cur+1} UNION ALL SELECT d+1 FROM days WHERE d<{cur+14})
SELECT d due_day, s.plan, (d-s.start_day) age, COUNT(*) n, ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price),2) mrr_due,
       ROUND(AVG(s.effective_price),2) avg_eff, ROUND(AVG(s.promotion),2) avg_promo
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id) JOIN days ON s.billing_day_mod30=(d%30)
WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.group_id='S2'
GROUP BY d,s.plan,age ORDER BY d,s.plan,age
""", 1000)
q('S2 due next 35 totals by week', f"""
WITH RECURSIVE days(d) AS (SELECT {cur+1} UNION ALL SELECT d+1 FROM days WHERE d<{cur+35}), due AS (
 SELECT d, s.effective_price*s.seat_count AS amt
 FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id) JOIN days ON s.billing_day_mod30=(d%30)
 WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.group_id='S2')
SELECT ((d-{cur+1})/7)+1 AS wk, COUNT(*) n, ROUND(SUM(amt),2) due_mrr FROM due GROUP BY wk ORDER BY wk
""", 20)

# Usage/cost by plan/group last 7 days approximate using current subscriptions join. daily_usage customer id, active/cancel included.
q('daily_usage last 7 by group current plan/status', f"""
SELECT c.group_id, COALESCE(s.plan,'?') plan, COALESCE(s.status,'?') status, COUNT(DISTINCT du.customer_id) customers,
       ROUND(SUM(du.usage_units),0) units
FROM daily_usage du JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
LEFT JOIN subscriptions s ON s.customer_id=du.customer_id AND du.day>=s.start_day AND (s.end_day IS NULL OR du.day<=s.end_day)
WHERE du.day BETWEEN {cur-6} AND {cur}
GROUP BY c.group_id,plan,status ORDER BY units DESC
""", 100)
q('service day last 21', f"""
SELECT day,total_usage_units,ROUND(p95_ms,1) p95,ROUND(error_rate,4) err,downtime_minutes,capacity_tier,capacity_units
FROM service_day WHERE day BETWEEN {cur-20} AND {cur} ORDER BY day
""", 100)

""", 200)

# Enterprise pipeline
q('latest open enterprise rows latest sender/day/group', """
WITH latest AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) maxmid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id)
SELECT et.sender, et.thread_type, et.day, c.group_id, COUNT(*) n, SUM(et.seat_count) seats, AVG(et.seat_count) avg_seats, MIN(et.seat_count) min_seats, MAX(et.seat_count) max_seats, AVG(et.turn_number) avg_turn
FROM latest l JOIN enterprise_turns et ON et.message_id=l.maxmid JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
GROUP BY et.sender, et.thread_type, et.day, c.group_id ORDER BY et.day DESC, et.sender, c.group_id
""", 500)
q('latest non-agent enterprise to answer', """
WITH latest AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) maxmid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id)
SELECT et.day, c.group_id, et.sender, et.thread_type, COUNT(*) n, SUM(et.seat_count) seats, MIN(et.seat_count) minseat, MAX(et.seat_count) maxseat
FROM latest l JOIN enterprise_turns et ON et.message_id=l.maxmid JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE et.sender!='agent'
GROUP BY et.day,c.group_id,et.sender,et.thread_type ORDER BY et.day DESC,c.group_id
""", 200)
q('enterprise accepted/rejected closed last 30', f"""
SELECT et.day,c.group_id,et.thread_type,et.close_reason,COUNT(*) n,SUM(et.seat_count) seats
FROM enterprise_turns et JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE et.closed=1 AND et.day BETWEEN {cur-30} AND {cur}
GROUP BY et.day,c.group_id,et.thread_type,et.close_reason ORDER BY et.day DESC,c.group_id
""", 300)
q('enterprise active subscriptions', """
SELECT s.customer_id,c.group_id,s.plan,s.seat_count,s.effective_price,s.promotion,s.start_day,s.billing_day_mod30,c.company_size_descriptor,c.company_primary_concern
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE c.customer_type='large' AND s.status='subscribed' ORDER BY s.seat_count DESC LIMIT 100
""", 120)
